Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(46 comments)

I think this patch is going in quite the wrong direction in many places. Rather than expanding the number of places that know what the paths are, it should be trying to remove all of them if possible. It would be ideal if inly lctl knew about them, so using get_param and set_param and list_param should be done everywhere if possible

It isn't an improvement if you just add all of the possible pathnames to all of the places that used to access /proc directly. 

It also looks like there is a bug in sanity in this patvh, since it failed three times.

Patch Set 1:

Andreas, Thank you for your detailed comments! I'll update the patch accordingly.

Patch Set 7:

Patch set 7 fixed the issues commented by James for Patch set 6.
1. ost-survey: fixed using lctl command
2. llobdstat.8: two lines removed
3. llstat: the patch added

4. For llstat and llobdstat, "lctl" is not used since (1) according to the options of llstat/llobdstat, the user may provide the full path rather than just the obd name; and (2) since the script periodically reads the file content, "lctl" would cause extra overhead compared with reading from an opened file.

parents.subject: LU-5276 build: handle RHEL ldiskfs series more accurated

commit.subject: LU-5030 utils: fix hard-coded /proc/fs/lustre in scripts
commit.message:
LU-5030 utils: fix hard-coded /proc/fs/lustre in scripts

In the upstream Linux kernel, the files under /proc/fs/lustre and lnet
will be moved in the future to use sysfs. Lustre handles this by
providing access to this data with the tool lctl which is independent
of where the data is located. Many scripts directly access the proc file
system instead of using lctl so this patch migrates those scripts to
do the proper thing.

Signed-off-by: Chao Wang <chao.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I1d96ccd27fee2b0eb0bf173a4e37adacb628f83c
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10534
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Minh Diep <minh.diep@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
